perturbation.original_text: 13.1 Compliance This agreement will be constructed in accordance with the laws of Papua New Guinea and the parties submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the National Court of Justice of Papua New Guinea.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 Compliance This agreement will be constructed in accordance with the laws as determined by Datec and the parties submit to the jurisdiction as determined by Datec.
perturbation.explanation: This removes the guarantee that the contract will be interpreted under Papua New Guinea law. This could lead to consumers being subjected to foreign laws or arbitrary interpretations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Consumer Protection Act of Papua New Guinea - Section Regarding Choice of Law
perturbation.law_citation: Consumer Protection Act (Papua New Guinea) - Choice of Law
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text violates the Consumer Protection Act by allowing Datec to arbitrarily determine which laws govern the agreement, potentially circumventing consumer protections mandated by Papua New Guinea law.

perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts consumer protection principles by allowing Datec to determine which laws govern the agreement. This removes the original guarantee that Papua New Guinea law would apply, potentially circumventing consumer protections. The original text ensures compliance with Papua New Guinea laws. The changed text allows Datec to choose the governing laws, creating a legal loophole and an omission of mandated consumer protection.

perturbation.original_text: 15.1 Indemnity By Datec PNG LTD Datec must indemnify, defend and hold harmless the BSP, their employees, agents, successors and assigns, from any and all Damages arising from, in connection with, or based on allegations of, any of the following: (a) Datec's breach of; (i) any representation and warranty specified in this agreement or (ii) its obligations with respect to BSP Confidential Information (b) any claim, demand, proceeding or other action ('Claim') arising out of or related to occurrences Datec is required to insure against (c) any Claim arising out of or in any way relating to: (i) Datec, (A) engaging a contractor; or (B) employing or terminating the employment of any person,; (ii) any fraudulent act or omission, or any fraudulent misrepresentation or deceit by Datec, its Personnel or subcontractors in connection with this agreement; or (iii) a decision by the BSP not to approve a subcontractor or to revoke its approval for an Approved Subcontractor under the agreement (d) any Claim whether or not the BSP is liable at law for the amount the subject of the Claim, which maybe made against the BSP by, or on behalf of: (i) the Tax Office in respect of any payroll tax or penalties that the office may seek to recover from the BSP in respect of any amounts paid to Datec under this agreement (ii) any revenue raising authority including the Commissioner of Taxation in respect of any other payment, liability, or penalty that the authority may seek to recover from the BSP in respect of any amounts paid to Datec under this agreement; (e) any Claim arising from: (i) the Acquired Assets or (ii) managed Third Party Agreements to which Datec is or becomes a party as a consequence of the operation of this agreement; and
perturbation.changed_text: 15.1 Indemnity By Datec PNG LTD Datec must indemnify BSP from Damages arising from Datec's breach of its obligations with respect to BSP Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This drastically limits the indemnity provided by Datec, removing key protections for BSP against various claims and liabilities, including those related to contractor engagements, fraudulent acts, and tax-related issues. Omitting these protections introduces legal risk.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act of Papua New Guinea - Section on Contractual Indemnities
perturbation.law_explanation: By severely limiting Datec's indemnity obligations, the modified text may contradict the principles of fair contractual indemnity as interpreted under the Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act, which ensures reasonable protection against liabilities arising from one party's actions or omissions.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: NATLEX - - Skip to Main Content Home Breadcrumb Title_of_text Main Region First Region Country(ies) Adopted on (Date of text)
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly reduces the scope of indemnity provided by Datec, which contradicts the principle of fair contractual indemnity. The original text provides comprehensive protection to BSP against various claims, including those related to contractor engagements, fraudulent acts, and tax issues. The omission of these protections creates a legal loophole, increasing the risk of liability for BSP.

perturbation.original_text: 5.7 Security And Occupational Health And Safety Datec must at all times comply with and ensure that its personnel and approved subcontractors comply with the laws and regulations on occupational health and safety.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.7 Security And Occupational Health And Safety Datec will make efforts to comply with the laws and regulations on occupational health and safety.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'must comply' to 'will make efforts to comply,' the obligation to adhere to occupational health and safety laws is weakened, potentially creating legal risk related to workplace safety standards.
perturbation.contradicted_law: National Occupational Health and Safety Policy of Papua New Guinea - Mandates strict compliance with OHS laws.
perturbation.law_citation: National OHS Policy (Papua New Guinea) - Compliance
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts the National Occupational Health and Safety Policy, which mandates strict adherence to OHS laws, whereas 'making efforts' allows for non-compliance and undermines the policy's objectives of ensuring workplace safety.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: NATLEX - - Skip to Main Content Home Breadcrumb Title_of_text Main Region First Region Country(ies) Adopted on (Date of text)
perturbation.justification: The original text states Datec 'must comply' with occupational health and safety laws, whereas the changed text weakens this to 'will make efforts to comply'. The Law Explanation states that the modified text contradicts the National Occupational Health and Safety Policy, which mandates strict adherence to OHS laws, and that 'making efforts' allows for non-compliance. The scraped law snippet 1 indicates a formal adoption process ('Adopted on (Date of text)'), suggesting the policy carries legal weight. The change from 'must comply' to 'will make efforts' creates a legal contradiction because it undermines the mandatory nature of the safety regulations, as the modified text allows for potential non-compliance. The perturbation definition describes the legal contradiction in this category as happening when a contract omits a legally mandated consumer protection.
